article_title: FTC and Eight States Settle With WPP, Publicis, and Dentsu Over Alleged Brand Safety Collusion, Ending the Industry's Coordinated Misinformation Floor
summary: Manual override: the substantive FTC story is well-sourced from the FTC primary release verbatim, but the historical-context paragraph misattributes specific facts to Fox Business that aren't in the snapshot, and includes an uncited 'Christchurch attack' / 2019 GARM-founding claim.

findings.message: Fox Business does not contain '100 member companies' or 'August 2024' specifics attributed to it
findings.details: Article states: 'According to Fox Business reporting, GARM grew to more than 100 member companies before the WFA announced in August 2024 that it was discontinuing the initiative, citing limited nonprofit resources to defend itself in court.' Fox Business source-2 (datePublished 2024-08-08) does NOT contain '100 member companies' or any specific membership count, and does NOT use the phrase 'August 2024' (it just reports the discontinuation announcement made 'Thursday' from the August 8 publication date). What Fox Business does support: WFA CEO Stephan Loerke 'discontinuing' GARM and 'GARM is a nonprofit with limited resources'. The '100 members' figure is real (well-documented in public reporting) but should be sourced from a publication that actually carries it, not attributed to Fox Business.

findings.message: Christchurch / 2019 GARM founding presented as fact without citation
findings.details: The article states: 'The Global Alliance for Responsible Media was launched by the World Federation of Advertisers in 2019 in the wake of the Christchurch attack, with the stated goal of helping advertisers avoid placement next to harmful content.' This sentence is uncited. None of the three cited sources (FTC, Bloomberg Law, Fox Business) mentions Christchurch or the 2019 founding date. The underlying historical facts are accurate, but the article presents them with implicit authority. Either add a sourced citation (the WFA's own announcement, GARM's Wikipedia entry, or a contemporaneous news report) or rephrase to remove the specifics.

content_preview.title: FTC and Eight States Settle With WPP, Publicis, and Dentsu Over Alleged Brand Safety Collusion, Ending the Industry's Coordinated Misinformation Floor
content_preview.summary: The Federal Trade Commission and a coalition of eight states secured a proposed consent order banning the three holding companies from coordinating brand safety standards or maintaining shared exclusion lists.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Three of the world's largest advertising holding companies have agreed to abandon coordinated brand safety standards under a proposed consent order with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and eight state attorneys general. The settlement, announced on April 15, 2026, resolves antitrust allegations that GroupM Worldwide LLC (operating as WPP Media), Publicis, Inc., and Dentsu US, Inc. conspired since 2018 to impose a uniform "Brand Safety Floor" that the FTC says distorted competition...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: The FTC core story (settlement, complaint, Ferguson quotes, GARM/APB framework) is solidly sourced verbatim from the FTC primary release. Two issues with the historical-context paragraph.

source_verification.https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/pre: source-0.html — FTC primary release. Verified verbatim: GroupM Worldwide LLC (operating as WPP Media), Publicis Inc., Dentsu US Inc., 'starting in 2018' collusion, 'Brand Safety Floor' phrase, GARM (Global Alliance for Responsible Media via WFA), APB (Advertiser Protection Bureau via 4As), 8 states (FL/IN/IA/MT/NE/TX/UT/WV), Northern District of Texas, Andrew N. Ferguson chairman quotes both verbatim ('The ad agencies' brand-safety conspiracy turned competition in the market for ad-buying services on its head'; 'antitrust laws guarantee participation in a market free from conduct, such as economic boycotts...'), 'Misinformation' designation, NewsGuard / Global Disinformation Index references. Notes Omnicom and IPG were primary competitors NOT in this complaint.
source_verification.https://news.bloomberglaw.com/antitrust/: source-1.html — SNAPSHOT EFFECTIVELY FAILED (78 lines, only page chrome and the title 'WPP, Publicis and Dentsu Settle Alleged Political Ad Boycotts'). The article's 'no admit, no deny' and 'behavioral commitments' attributions to Bloomberg Law cannot be verified against the snapshot, but they are standard FTC consent-order language and are consistent with the FTC primary source's structure.
source_verification.https://www.foxbusiness.com/media/ad-gro: source-2.html (datePublished 2024-08-08). Verified verbatim: WFA/GARM antitrust suit by X+Rumble, defendants list (CVS Health, Mars, Orsted, Unilever), Linda Yaccarino 'billions of dollars' claim, House Judiciary Committee 'directly organized boycotts' quote, WFA CEO Stephan Loerke 'discontinuing' announcement, 'nonprofit with limited resources'. NOT in source-2: '100 member companies' specific count, 'August 2024' phrase (date implicit from publication date but not used), 'Christchurch'.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: FTC core facts verified verbatim. Two issues with the historical-context paragraph: (1) '100 member companies' attributed to Fox Business but absent from the snapshot; (2) 'launched in 2019 in the wake of the Christchurch attack' presented as fact without citation, not in any cited snapshot.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The Analysis section's framing of the case as 'antitrust rather than speech' is editorial but defensible.
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered. Distinct from prior FTC and platform-policy articles in the archive.
concerns: '100 member companies' attributed to Fox Business but not present in the snapshot.
'Christchurch attack' / 2019 GARM founding date presented without citation.
recommendations: Re-source the '100 member companies' figure from a publication that actually carries it (the WFA's own materials, contemporary press, or an industry trade publication), or remove the specific number.
Either source the 'Christchurch attack' / 2019 founding sentence with a real citation, or rephrase to remove the specifics (the article doesn't need this paragraph to make its case).
All other historical context (Loerke discontinuation, Yaccarino billions, House Judiciary directly-organized-boycotts quote, X+Rumble suit, defendant list) is solid.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REQUEST_CHANGES. The substantive FTC settlement story is fully verified. Two surgical fixes needed in the GARM-history paragraph: re-source or remove '100 member companies' and the Christchurch/2019 founding context.
